heading: Jurisdiction of district courts of United States
label: 6203 Jurisdiction of district courts of United States
notes: References in TextThe Federal Rules of Civil Procedure, referred to in subsec. (b)(2)(C), are classified to Title 28, Appendix, Judiciary and Judicial Procedure.

children.text: in determining whether a person has violated or is about to violate any of the foreign antitrust laws administered or enforced by the foreign antitrust authority, or
in enforcing any of such foreign antitrust laws.

children.heading: Use of appointee to receive evidence
children.label: 1 Use of appointee to receive evidence
A
children.text: An order issued under subsection (a) may direct that testimony or a statement be given, or a document or other thing be produced, to a person who shall be recommended by the Attorney General and appointed by the court.

children.label: B
children.text: A person appointed under subparagraph (A) shall have power to administer any necessary oath and to take such testimony or such statement.

children.label: A
children.text: An order issued under subsection (a) may prescribe the practice and procedure for taking testimony and statements and for producing documents and other things.

children.label: B
children.text: Such practice and procedure may be in whole or in part the practice and procedure of the foreign state, or the regional economic integration organization, represented by the foreign antitrust authority with respect to which the Attorney General requests such order.

children.label: C
children.text: To the extent such order does not prescribe otherwise, any testimony and statements required to be taken shall be taken, and any documents and other things required to be produced shall be produced, in accordance with the Federal Rules of Civil Procedure.

children.heading: Rights and privileges preserved
children.label: c Rights and privileges preserved
children.text: A person may not be compelled under an order issued under subsection (a) to give testimony or a statement, or to produce a document or other thing, in violation of any legally applicable right or privilege.

children.heading: Voluntary conduct
children.label: d Voluntary conduct
children.text: This section does not preclude a person in the United States from voluntarily giving testimony or a statement, or producing a document or other thing, in any manner acceptable to such person for use in an investigation by a foreign antitrust authority.
